original_reasoning: شعفة=love that overwhelms/covers the heart from above (غشّى الحبّ القلب). διαλυσίφιλος=dissolving friendship/love. Both are about love's transformative power - one overwhelming, the other dissolving. The love domain is shared but the direction differs.

Arabic qa'thara = uprooting, pulling up from the root. Greek helkethron = stock/sole of the plough (from helko 'drag, draw, pull'). Both center on dragging-up-from-soil action. Strong concrete link: uprooting tool meets ploughing tool, both pull through earth.
الطنس is intense darkness and λανθάνουσα is hidden/escaping notice; both share the mafhum of concealment and obscurity.
Both sides point to a specific place-name; this looks like toponymic preservation more than ordinary lexical meaning.
Arabic khandaris = aged date/wine, marked as foreign loan by ibn Durayd. Greek chondros = groats, lump. Direct loan etymology — khandaris is from chondros derivative. Strong borrowing link.
هدن=truce/calm; εὗδον=sleeping. Possible semantic link via rest/quiet

التناوه=ترك المذاكرة (abandoning discussion/study). καταλύω=to dissolve, destroy, bring to an end. Abandoning discussion IS a dissolution/ending of the activity. The cessation concept maps well: ترك (leaving off) parallels κατα-λύω (un-doing).
Fettering and binding can extend to what is fixed or anchored like a mountain mass.
